actors.name: Berachain User
actors.description: Individuals interacting with the Berachain ecosystem. This includes Liquidity Providers, $BGT Holders/Delegators, dApp users (BEX, Honey, BeraHub), NFT holders, and governance participants.
capabilities: Connect wallet to Berachain
Acquire and hold $BERA, $BGT, $HONEY, NFTs
Provide liquidity to BEX pools
Stake receipt tokens in Reward Vaults
Delegate (boost) $BGT to Validators
Claim $BGT rewards from Reward Vaults
Claim Incentive Tokens from boosting Validators
Mint and redeem $HONEY
Create and vote on governance proposals
Redeem $BGT for $BERA
Bridge assets to/from Berachain
Trade tokens on BEX

actors.name: Berachain Validator
actors.description: Entities operating nodes, staking $BERA to secure the network, proposing blocks, participating in consensus, directing $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults, and earning rewards ($BGT, transaction fees, incentives). Chosen based on $BERA stake (top 69).
capabilities: Stake $BERA (min 250k, max 10M)
Operate a validator node
Propose blocks and participate in consensus
Receive base $BGT emissions for block production
Receive variable $BGT emissions based on boost
Direct $BGT emissions to whitelisted Reward Vaults
Receive protocol-provided Incentives from Reward Vaults
Set commission rate on earned Incentives
Distribute Incentives to $BGT delegators

actors.name: Protocol / Ecosystem Project
actors.description: Entities or teams deploying decentralized applications (dApps) and smart contracts on Berachain. They can create Reward Vaults and offer Incentive Tokens to attract liquidity and $BGT emissions.
capabilities: Deploy smart contracts and dApps on Berachain
Create Reward Vaults (permissionlessly)
Submit governance proposals to whitelist Reward Vaults
Manage Incentive Tokens for their Reward Vaults (if designated as Token Manager)
Offer Incentive Tokens to attract $BGT emissions

actors.name: Guardian
actors.description: A 5-of-9 multisig with elected signers responsible for overseeing governance proposals during the timelock period. They can cancel malicious proposals.
capabilities: Monitor governance proposals during timelock
Cancel malicious proposals via multisig action

actors.name: Token Manager
actors.description: A wallet address designated (via governance) to manage and offer specific Incentive Tokens for a particular Reward Vault. Can set incentive rates and deposit tokens.
capabilities: Offer whitelisted Incentive Tokens to a specific Reward Vault
Set the distribution rate for Incentive Tokens per $BGT
Deposit Incentive Tokens into the Reward Vault's incentive pool
Increase incentive rate when depositing additional tokens

assets.name: $BERA Token
assets.description: Berachain's native gas token, used for transaction fees and validator staking. Can be obtained by burning $BGT.

assets.name: $BGT Token (Bera Governance Token)
assets.description: Berachain's non-transferrable governance and staking/reward delegation token. Earned by participating in Proof-of-Liquidity. Used for voting, boosting validators, and can be redeemed for $BERA.

assets.name: $HONEY Token
assets.description: Berachain's native, fully collateralized stablecoin, soft-pegged to the US Dollar. Minted by depositing whitelisted collateral.

assets.name: Receipt Tokens
assets.description: Tokens representing a user's position in a liquidity pool (e.g., BEX LP tokens) or other PoL-eligible activities. Staked in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT.

assets.name: Incentive Tokens
assets.description: Whitelisted ERC20 tokens offered by Protocols/Token Managers in Reward Vaults to Validators (and their delegators) in exchange for directing $BGT emissions.

assets.name: Berachain NFTs
assets.description: Official NFT collections (e.g., Bong Bears) on Ethereum, bridgeable to Berachain. Used for airdrop eligibility and community identity.

assets.name: Governance Proposal Data
assets.description: Data defining a proposal for changes to Berachain's PoL, dApps, or parameters, including its state (pending, active, succeeded, defeated, executed).

assets.name: User Private Keys
assets.description: Cryptographic keys held by users in their wallets, used to authorize transactions and interactions on Berachain.

assets.name: Transaction Data
assets.description: Information about transactions submitted to the Berachain network, including sender, receiver, value, and calldata.

assets.name: Validator $BERA Stake
assets.description: $BERA tokens staked by Validators to participate in consensus and secure the network.

assets.name: Delegated $BGT (Boosts)
assets.description: $BGT tokens delegated by users to Validators to increase their $BGT reward emissions.

assets.name: Reward Vault Deposits
assets.description: PoL-eligible receipt tokens staked by users in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT.

assets.name: Collateral for $HONEY
assets.description: Whitelisted assets (e.g., $USDC, $BYUSD) deposited into HoneyFactory vaults to mint $HONEY.

assets.name: $BGT Emissions
assets.description: Newly minted $BGT tokens distributed as rewards for block production (base to validator, variable to Reward Vaults based on boost).

assets.name: Protocol-provided Incentives
assets.description: Incentive Tokens distributed by Reward Vaults to Validators (and their boosters) for $BGT emissions directed to them.

assets.name: dApp Fees
assets.description: Fees collected from Berachain core dApps (BEX, Honey) and distributed to $BGT boosters via the FeeCollector contract.

assets.name: Bridged Assets (Tokens/NFTs)
assets.description: Assets transferred to/from Berachain from/to other blockchains via the Berachain Bridges.

components.name: Berachain Network
components.description: The EVM-Identical Layer 1 blockchain, utilizing Proof-of-Liquidity and built on BeaconKit. Hosts all smart contracts and processes transactions.

components.name: BeaconKit
components.description: The modular consensus layer framework used by Berachain, integrating CometBFT consensus for single slot finality.

components.name: BEX (Berachain Exchange)
components.description: Berachain's native decentralized exchange (DEX) for swapping tokens and providing liquidity. Consists of smart contracts for pools and a user-facing dApp.

components.name: BeraHub
components.description: A central dApp for managing $BGT (boosting, redeeming, claiming), accessing BEX liquidity, participating in governance, and viewing Reward Vaults/Validators.

components.name: Reward Vault Smart Contracts
components.description: Smart contracts where users stake PoL-eligible assets (Receipt Tokens) to earn $BGT emissions directed by Validators. Can also offer Incentive Tokens.

components.name: Honey dApp
components.description: User interface for interacting with the HoneyFactory to mint/redeem $HONEY and for swapping approved stables for $HONEY (Honey Swap).

components.name: HoneyFactory Smart Contract
components.description: The core contract for minting and burning $HONEY. Manages collateral vaults and $HONEY supply.

components.name: Berachain Governance Contracts
components.description: Smart contracts (e.g., Governor, Timelock) that manage the on-chain governance lifecycle, including proposal creation, voting, and execution.

components.name: User Wallet Software
components.description: Client-side software (e.g., MetaMask, Rabby) used by users to manage private keys, sign transactions, and interact with Berachain dApps and contracts.

components.name: Validator Node & Staking Infrastructure
components.description: The hardware and software stack run by Berachain Validators to participate in consensus, propose blocks, and manage their $BERA stake.

components.name: Berachain Bridge
components.description: Canonical bridge (powered by LayerZero) for transferring tokens between Berachain and other supported blockchains. Includes smart contracts and a frontend.

components.name: Berachain NFT Bridge
components.description: Bridge for transferring official Berachain NFT collections between Ethereum and Berachain. Includes smart contracts and a frontend.

components.name: BGTIncentiveDistributor Smart Contract
components.description: Contract responsible for managing the distribution and claiming of Incentive Tokens earned by users who boost Validators.

components.name: FeeCollector Smart Contract
components.description: Contract that collects fees from core Berachain dApps (BEX, Honey) and distributes them (as $WBERA) to $BGT boosters.

components.name: Distributor Smart Contract
components.description: Contract responsible for minting and distributing $BGT block rewards (variable portion) to whitelisted Reward Vaults based on Validator allocations.

components.name: Guardian Multisig
components.description: A 5-of-9 multisig mechanism (on-chain contract controlled by off-chain coordinated signers) used by Guardians to cancel malicious governance proposals during timelock.

components.name: RPC Endpoints
components.description: Network interface allowing wallets and applications to read blockchain data and submit transactions to the Berachain network.

data_flows.description: User expresses intent to interact with Berachain via their Wallet Software.
User Wallet Software submits a signed transaction to Berachain Network via RPC Endpoints.
RPC Endpoints relay transaction to the Berachain Network for consensus and execution.
Berachain Network executes user transactions interacting with BEX smart contracts.
Berachain Network executes user transactions interacting with Reward Vault Smart Contracts.
Berachain Network executes user transactions for $BGT management via BeraHub interactions (which call relevant contracts).
Berachain Network executes user transactions with HoneyFactory for $HONEY operations.
Berachain Network executes user transactions for governance participation.
Validator stakes $BERA via their Validator Node Infrastructure.
Validator Node Infrastructure participates in Berachain consensus.
Berachain Network rewards block-proposing Validator with base $BGT and fees.
Validator (via their infra) configures Distributor contract for $BGT emission direction.
Distributor contract sends $BGT emissions to chosen Reward Vaults.
Reward Vaults transfer earned Incentives to BGTIncentiveDistributor for boosters.
User claims their share of incentives from BGTIncentiveDistributor.
Protocol submits a governance proposal to whitelist a Reward Vault.

data_flows.data: Incentive Token (Asset 5) deposit and rate setting by Token Manager (Actor 5)
data_flows.description: Token Manager adds/configures incentives for a Reward Vault.
Guardians interact with their Multisig to cancel a governance proposal.
Guardian Multisig executes cancellation on the Governance Contract.
User interacts with Berachain Bridge to transfer tokens.
User interacts with Berachain NFT Bridge to transfer NFTs.
BEX contract sends collected fees to FeeCollector contract.
HoneyFactory contract sends collected fees to FeeCollector contract.
FeeCollector distributes collected fees (as $WBERA) to users who boosted validators.

trust_boundaries.name: User - User Wallet Software
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the human user and the wallet software they use. Trust in the wallet to correctly represent user intent and securely manage keys.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet Software - dApp Frontend
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary when a user's wallet connects to and interacts with a dApp frontend (e.g., BeraHub, BEX UI). Trust in dApp frontend to correctly display information and formulate transactions.

trust_boundaries.name: dApp Frontend - Berachain Smart Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary where a dApp frontend (e.g., BeraHub) initiates transactions with on-chain smart contracts (e.g., BEX pools, Reward Vaults). Relies on RPC for submission.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet Software - RPC Endpoint
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary when wallet software submits transactions or queries state via an RPC endpoint.

trust_boundaries.name: RPC Endpoint - Berachain Network
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the RPC service and the actual Berachain P2P network and consensus.

trust_boundaries.name: Validator Node Infrastructure - Berachain Network
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between a validator's privately managed infrastructure and the public Berachain network. Trust in validator to operate honestly and securely.

trust_boundaries.name: Protocol/Ecosystem Project - Berachain Network
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary when a protocol deploys or interacts with its smart contracts (e.g., Reward Vaults, incentive mechanisms) on Berachain.

trust_boundaries.name: Guardian Signers - Guardian Multisig Contract
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the off-chain coordination/decision of Guardian signers and the on-chain execution via the Guardian Multisig contract.

trust_boundaries.name: Berachain Network - External Blockchain (via Bridge)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary when assets are transferred between Berachain and another blockchain using the Berachain Bridge (e.g., LayerZero). Trust in bridge security.

behaviors.name: User Connects Wallet to Berachain dApp
behaviors.description: User uses Wallet Software (C9) to connect to a Berachain dApp (e.g., BeraHub C4, BEX C3).

behaviors.name: User Provides Liquidity to BEX
behaviors.description: User, via their Wallet (C9), submits a transaction to add liquidity to a BEX (C3) pool, receiving Receipt Tokens (A4).

behaviors.name: User Stakes Receipt Tokens in Reward Vault
behaviors.description: User, via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), stakes Receipt Tokens (A4) into a Reward Vault (C5) to earn $BGT (A2).

behaviors.name: User Delegates (Boosts) $BGT to Validator
behaviors.description: User, via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), delegates their $BGT (A2) to a Berachain Validator (A2) to boost their emissions and earn Incentives (A5). Target is BeraHub which facilitates interaction with staking contracts.

behaviors.name: Validator Proposes Block
behaviors.description: Berachain Validator (A2), using their Node Infrastructure (C10), proposes a new block to the Berachain Network (C1).

behaviors.name: Validator Receives $BGT Block Rewards
behaviors.description: Berachain Network (C1) distributes base $BGT emissions (A14) to the block-proposing Validator (A2).

behaviors.name: Validator Directs $BGT Emissions to Reward Vaults
behaviors.description: Berachain Validator (A2) configures the Distributor contract (C15) to direct their variable $BGT emissions (A14) to selected Reward Vaults (C5).

behaviors.name: Protocol/Token Manager Offers Incentives to Reward Vault
behaviors.description: Token Manager (A5) for a Protocol (A3) adds Incentive Tokens (A5) to a Reward Vault (C5) to attract $BGT emissions.

behaviors.name: User Claims $BGT from Reward Vault
behaviors.description: User, via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), claims earned $BGT (A2) from a Reward Vault (C5).

behaviors.name: User Claims Incentives from Boosting
behaviors.description: User, via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), claims earned Incentive Tokens (A5, A15) from the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract (C13).

behaviors.name: User Mints $HONEY
behaviors.description: User, via Honey dApp (C6) and Wallet (C9), deposits Collateral (A13) into HoneyFactory (C7) to mint $HONEY (A3).

behaviors.name: User Redeems $HONEY
behaviors.description: User, via Honey dApp (C6) and Wallet (C9), burns $HONEY (A3) at HoneyFactory (C7) to receive Collateral (A13).

behaviors.name: User Creates Governance Proposal
behaviors.description: User (with sufficient $BGT), via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), submits a Governance Proposal (A7) to Berachain Governance Contracts (C8).

behaviors.name: User Votes on Governance Proposal
behaviors.description: User (with $BGT), via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), casts a vote on an active Governance Proposal (A7) using Berachain Governance Contracts (C8).

behaviors.name: User Redeems $BGT for $BERA
behaviors.description: User, via BeraHub (C4) and Wallet (C9), burns $BGT (A2) to receive $BERA (A1) 1:1. Target is BeraHub which facilitates interaction with relevant contract.

behaviors.name: Validator Stakes $BERA
behaviors.description: Validator (A2) stakes $BERA (A1, A10) on the Berachain Network (C1) to become eligible for block production.

behaviors.name: Guardian Cancels Malicious Proposal
behaviors.description: Guardians (A4) use their Multisig (C16) to cancel a malicious Governance Proposal (A7) during its timelock period in the Governance Contracts (C8).

behaviors.name: User Bridges Assets via Berachain Bridge
behaviors.description: User interacts with the Berachain Bridge (C11) to transfer tokens (A17) to or from Berachain.

behaviors.name: User Bridges NFTs via Berachain NFT Bridge
behaviors.description: User interacts with the Berachain NFT Bridge (C12) to transfer official Berachain NFTs (A6, A17) between Ethereum and Berachain.
